meta.description: Flash Info source registry — domain-organised, weekend supplement priority
meta.priority_order: Weekend supplements first — all other domains — cinema last
meta.non_rss_note: Wikipedia and Internet Archive handled via Python scraper — see scraper_targets block

sources.label: Aeon Magazine
sources.why: Best cross-domain ideas source. Philosophy, psychology, science — analogy goldmine. Weekend supplement quality.

sources.label: Psyche Magazine (Aeon sister)
sources.why: Psychology and philosophy of the human condition. Practical essays. Strong hook material.

sources.label: Arts and Letters Daily
sources.why: Curated digest of best ideas writing globally. Philosophy, literature, history, culture.

sources.label: Indian Philosophy Blog
sources.why: Indian philosophical traditions ancient to modern. Rare English-language source on Indian thought.

sources.label: Open Culture
sources.why: Free cultural and educational media. Philosophy, lectures, historical documents made accessible.

sources.label: Nautilus Magazine
sources.why: Best long-form science narrative. Every article builds to a philosophical insight. Analogy-rich.

sources.label: Quanta Magazine
sources.why: Math, physics, biology written for intelligent non-scientists. Exceptional clarity. Completely free.

sources.label: The Guardian — Science
sources.why: Accessible science with cultural angle. Free, high quality, updated daily.

sources.label: BBC Future
sources.why: Accessible science with cultural angle. Strong sentence hooks. High surprising-fact density.

sources.label: ScienceDaily — Mind and Brain
sources.why: Research news for non-scientists. Mind, behaviour, neuroscience — hook material.

sources.label: Knowable Magazine
sources.why: Science journalism from peer-reviewed research. Rigorous but accessible. Free.

sources.label: World History Encyclopedia
sources.why: Best free world history resource. Non-profit, peer-reviewed, accessible writing.

sources.label: BBC History
sources.why: Popular history writing. Surprising facts and counterintuitive takes on well-known events.

sources.label: Smithsonian Magazine
sources.why: History, science, culture crossovers. Weekend supplement equivalent. Rare cross-domain examples.

sources.label: History Today
sources.why: British history magazine. Narrative approach. Good analogy potential from obscure historical episodes.

sources.label: History Hit
sources.why: Accessible history stories. Good counterintuitive fact density.

sources.label: Damn Interesting
sources.why: Long-form articles on obscure true stories from history, science, psychology. Every article earns the name.

sources.label: Live History India
sources.why: Best Indian history storytelling in English. Culture, heritage, places, people. Weekend supplement quality.

sources.label: Puratattva — Indian Heritage Architecture
sources.why: Indian history through architecture and heritage sites. Specialist knowledge, accessible writing.

sources.label: Madras Courier
sources.why: Chennai-focused. Culture, history, society. High relevance for Tamil Nadu content.

sources.label: Sanskriti Magazine
sources.why: Indian culture and heritage — history, traditions, civilisation. Free RSS.

sources.label: Indian History Blog
sources.why: Deep Indian history ancient to modern. Well-researched individual blogger.

sources.label: Navrangi India — Ancient Indian History
sources.why: Ancient Indian culture, architecture, temples, monuments. Blog by retired geologist.

sources.label: Vikatan RSS
sources.why: Tamil magazine group. Headlines from Ananda Vikatan, Junior Vikatan, Aval Vikatan. Free RSS tier only.

sources.label: Dinamalar
sources.why: Tamil daily newspaper. Literary and cultural sections on weekends.

sources.label: Dinamani
sources.why: Tamil newspaper with literary supplement. Good Sunday feature coverage.

sources.label: Puthiya Thalaimurai
sources.why: Tamil news and culture channel. Feature content available free.

sources.label: Tamil Murasu Singapore
sources.why: Global Tamil perspective from Singapore. Broader diaspora cultural angle.

sources.label: Tamil Oneindia
sources.why: Tamil culture, lifestyle, society. Mix of Tamil and English feature content.

sources.label: Aeon — Language
sources.why: Philosophy of language, linguistics, how language shapes thought. Excellent analogy material.

sources.label: Language Log
sources.why: Academic linguists writing for general public. Language, culture, usage, etymology. Surprising facts daily.

sources.label: OED Word of the Day
sources.why: Etymology and word histories. Surprising origin stories for common words.

sources.label: Psyche Magazine
sources.why: Psychology and human condition essays. Practical and philosophical. Strong hook material.

sources.label: Cognition Today
sources.why: Cognitive science made accessible. How people think, decide, perceive. Indian blogger.

sources.label: ScienceDaily — Psychology
sources.why: Latest psychology research translated for general readers. Good for counterintuitive findings.

sources.label: BPS Research Digest
sources.why: Psychology research distilled into readable summaries. High surprising finding density.

sources.label: Greater Good Magazine Berkeley
sources.why: Science of wellbeing, compassion, human connection. Accessible research summaries.

sources.label: It's Nice That
sources.why: Design and visual culture. Cross-domain creativity hooks. Globally respected publication.

sources.label: Designboom
sources.why: World's first online design magazine. Architecture, design, art, technology intersections.

sources.label: Eye on Design AIGA
sources.why: Design culture and criticism. Visual communication, typography, graphic design essays.

sources.label: Print Magazine
sources.why: Visual culture and design history. Cultural impact of design writing.

sources.label: My Modern Met
sources.why: Art, design, photography, inspiring stories. Cross-domain positive culture content.

sources.label: Serious Eats
sources.why: Food science and cooking as systems thinking. Rich in process analogies.

sources.label: Down to Earth — Food Agriculture
sources.why: India food systems, agriculture, environment. Relevant local context.

sources.label: Civil Eats
sources.why: Food systems, agriculture policy, sustainability. Good cross-domain analogies.

sources.label: ArchDaily
sources.why: World's most visited architecture resource. Projects, interviews, ideas.

sources.label: Dezeen
sources.why: Architecture, interiors, design news. Cultural context for built environment.

sources.label: SAH Blog — Architectural Historians
sources.why: History of architecture — cross-cultural, historical analogies.

sources.label: Landscape Architecture Magazine
sources.why: Landscape, cities, built environment. Nature-human interface writing.

sources.label: Pitchfork — News
sources.why: Music criticism and cultural context. Long-form music essays with strong cultural hooks.

sources.label: The Wire Magazine
sources.why: Experimental and world music writing. Indian classical, jazz, global music culture.

sources.label: Carnatic Music Corner
sources.why: Indian classical music analysis. Deep cultural context for Tamil music tradition.

sources.label: Aeon — Economics
sources.why: Economics through philosophical and cultural lens. Counterintuitive takes on money and systems.

sources.label: Economic and Political Weekly
sources.why: Rigorous India economics, politics, society. Academic quality, accessible essays.

sources.label: Naanayam Vikatan
sources.why: Tamil personal finance and business. Accessible economics for Tamil readers.

sources.label: Atanu Dey — Deeshaa
sources.why: Indian development economics and systems thinking. Contrarian takes.

sources.label: The Atlantic
sources.why: Long-form ideas and cultural essays. Society, identity. Counterintuitive takes.

sources.label: The Caravan Magazine
sources.why: India's best long-form narrative journalism. Culture, politics, society. Weekend supplement quality.

sources.label: Scroll.in Arts and Culture
sources.why: Indian arts, culture, ideas. Supplement-quality writing on society and identity.

sources.label: The Wire India
sources.why: Indian culture, media, science. Quality opinion on society and identity.

sources.label: Open Democracy
sources.why: Global ideas on democracy, culture, identity. Counterintuitive and underrepresented perspectives.

sources.label: Nieman Lab Harvard
sources.why: Journalism futures, media business, digital culture. Meta-content for media professionals.

sources.label: afaqs India
sources.why: Indian media and advertising industry. Relevant to content strategy and broadcast context.

sources.label: Columbia Journalism Review
sources.why: Journalism culture and media criticism. Press freedom, editorial craft, industry analysis.

sources.label: Poynter
sources.why: Journalism practice and media ethics. Practical craft writing.

sources.label: Wired
sources.why: Technology-culture crossovers. AI, systems, future of work. Cultural angle not technical specs.

sources.label: MIT Technology Review
sources.why: Technology ideas with cultural context. AI ethics, implications, society. Free articles.

sources.label: The Markup
sources.why: Technology accountability journalism. How systems affect society. Free, non-profit.

sources.label: Fast Company
sources.why: Creativity, systems thinking, work culture. Cross-domain hooks on technology and organisations.

sources.label: arXiv CS AI
sources.why: AI research headlines. Useful for AI-in-filmmaking content track. Titles and abstracts only.

sources.label: Harvard Business Review
sources.why: Management ideas with analogy potential. Systems thinking applied to organisations.

sources.label: Atlas Obscura
sources.why: Wonders, strange places, hidden history. Every article is a flash snippet candidate.

sources.label: Amusing Planet
sources.why: Fascinating places, cultural curiosities, geographical surprises. Perfect flash snippet source.

sources.label: eNidhi India
sources.why: South India travel writing from Chennai perspective. Culturally grounded.

sources.label: Be on the Road
sources.why: India travel writing. Regional cultural context.

sources.label: The Guardian Books
sources.why: Book reviews and literary essays with strong analytical sentences. Free, updated daily.

sources.label: Anuradha Goyal Book Reviews
sources.why: Top Indian book review blogger. Literature, travel, culture. Well-curated.

sources.label: Frontline Books and Ideas
sources.why: India's top magazine. Books, ideas, culture sections. Weekend supplement equivalent.

sources.label: The Hindu Books
sources.why: Indian literary criticism. Books reviewed with cultural context.

sources.label: Baradwaj Rangan Film and Books Blog
sources.why: India's best English film critic. Cross-domain analogy-rich writing. Confirmed active March 2026.

sources.label: Film Companion South
sources.why: Indian film analysis. High writing quality. Last priority domain per brief.

sources.label: NDTV Movies
sources.why: Indian film news. Last priority domain. Included for completeness.

sources.label: The Guardian Sport
sources.why: Sports writing as social and cultural commentary. Identity, society, politics through sport.

sources.label: Wisden India Cricket Culture
sources.why: Cricket as Indian cultural lens. Long-form essays on cricket, society, history.

sources.label: Scroll.in Sports Field
sources.why: Indian sports journalism with cultural depth. Good cross-domain hook source.

sources.label: Down to Earth
sources.why: India environment, science, society. Strong factual hooks relevant to Indian context.

sources.label: The Guardian Environment
sources.why: Environment news and analysis with cultural and societal context. Free, updated daily.

sources.label: Mongabay India
sources.why: India-specific environment and nature journalism. Strong local ecological writing.

sources.label: Yale Environment 360
sources.why: Global environment features. Long-form essays on ecology, climate, nature.

sources.label: Atlas Obscura
sources.why: THE source for wonders and strange facts. 20000+ curious places and stories. Every item is a potential flash snippet.

sources.label: Futility Closet
sources.why: 12000+ entertaining curiosities in history, literature, language, art, philosophy, mathematics. Updated daily. Pure Hook Bank material.

sources.label: Amusing Planet
sources.why: Fascinating places, cultural oddities, geographical surprises. High wonder-density writing.

sources.label: Mental Floss
sources.why: Fascinating facts, trivia, weird history. Bite-sized, highly readable. Excellent hook material.

sources.label: Smithsonian Smart News
sources.why: Daily surprising science and history news. Short, factual, wonder-inducing items.

sources.label: Today I Found Out
sources.why: The backstory behind interesting facts. High hook density.

sources.label: Ripley's Believe It or Not
sources.why: Strange facts, records, unusual phenomena. Pure wow-factor content.

sources.label: Frontline Magazine
sources.why: India's best long-form investigative magazine. Closest English equivalent to a serious weekend supplement.

sources.label: New Indian Express
sources.why: South India English paper. Strong Sunday supplement. Direct replacement for one of the four physical magazines.

sources.label: The News Minute
sources.why: South India news and culture. Tamil Nadu culture, society, media. High local relevance.

sources.label: Outlook India Culture
sources.why: Indian news magazine culture and society sections. Supplement equivalent.

sources.label: The Hindu Opinion
sources.why: India's newspaper of record. Opinion and ideas section. Free RSS.

sources.label: Indian Express Ideas
sources.why: Ideas section equivalent to Sunday supplement. High quality opinion and analysis.

sources.label: Futility Closet
sources.why: 12000+ entertaining curiosities from history, literature, language, art, philosophy, mathematics. Pure cross-domain wow material.

sources.label: Quanta Magazine
sources.why: Math, physics, biology written for intelligent non-scientists. Exceptional clarity. Free.

sources.label: Damn Interesting
sources.why: Long-form true stories from history and science. Every article earns the name.

sources.label: Today I Found Out
sources.why: The backstory behind surprising facts. High hook density.

sources.label: BPS Research Digest
sources.why: Psychology research distilled into readable summaries. Counterintuitive finding density.

sources.label: Ancient History Encyclopedia RSS
sources.why: Peer-reviewed world history. Rich in cross-domain analogies from ancient civilisations.

sources.label: Knowable Magazine
sources.why: Science journalism from Annual Reviews. Rigorous but accessible.

sources.label: Core77 Design
sources.why: Industrial design and craft. Process thinking, making, problem solving analogies.

sources.label: Hakai Magazine
sources.why: Coastal science and society. Rich natural world analogies.

sources.label: The Ringer
sources.why: Sports culture and society writing. Cross-domain sports analogies.

sources.label: Famous Scientists
sources.why: Narrative biographies of scientists with episodes and anecdotes. Ramanujan, C.V. Raman, Edison, Curie, Tesla, Newton, Darwin.

sources.label: Science History Institute
sources.why: In-depth narrative profiles of chemists, physicists, life scientists with historical context. Free, no paywall.

sources.label: Nobel Prize Biographies
sources.why: Official biographies and lectures of all Nobel laureates. Free, authoritative, narrative style.

sources.label: MacTutor History of Mathematics
sources.why: Scholarly story-driven biographies of mathematicians from ancient to modern. Deep historical context. Free.

sources.label: IAS Resonance Journal
sources.why: Indian Academy of Sciences. Classics and Masterclass series on scientific breakthroughs. Profiles of Indian scientists with personal reminiscences. Free open access.

sources.label: Vigyan Prasar
sources.why: Indian government science communication. Biographies of Indian scientists. Homi Bhabha, C.V. Raman series. Free.

sources.label: The Better India - Innovation
sources.why: Story-based articles on Indian grassroots innovators. Jugaad innovation, rural problem-solving, Tamil Nadu achievers.

sources.label: Internet Encyclopedia of Philosophy
sources.why: Ancient Greek philosophy, Socrates, Plato, Aristotle, Nagarjuna, Chanakya. Story-driven contextual articles. Free, no paywall.

sources.label: The Famous People
sources.why: Anecdote-filled bios covering philosophers, scientists, rebels, writers, artists, explorers. Strong on Indian figures.

sources.label: Biography Online
sources.why: Narrative biographies with key life episodes. Shakespeare, Tagore, da Vinci, Tesla, revolutionaries. Free.

sources.label: Cultural India
sources.why: Bhagat Singh, Indian leaders, cultural icons with narrative story format. Free.

sources.label: Nelson Mandela Foundation
sources.why: Episode-by-episode narrative biography of Mandela. Robben Island, freedom struggle. Free.

sources.label: National Women's History Museum
sources.why: Rosa Parks, women scientists, rebels. Story format with episodes and activism context. Free.

sources.label: Tamil Nation Heritage
sources.why: Thiruvalluvar, Subramania Bharati, Kalki, M.S. Subbulakshmi, Ilayaraja. Cultural episodes and facts. Free.

sources.label: Madras Musings Archive
sources.why: Chennai/Madras history, Tamil culture stories. Long-form narrative. G.D. Naidu, Tamil icons. Free archive.

sources.label: Tamilians.in
sources.why: Tamil Nadu achievers and inventors in Tamil language. Story profiles. Free.

sources.label: Popular Archaeology
sources.why: Quarterly online magazine on global archaeology. Lost cities, prehistoric sites. Narrative-driven. Free.

sources.label: Archaeology Magazine
sources.why: Archaeological discoveries worldwide. Story-format features. Free web articles.

sources.label: Sahapedia
sources.why: Indian heritage platform. Tamil Nadu/Pallava/Chola studies, megaliths, M.S. Subbulakshmi, Raja Ravi Varma. Narrative modules. Free.

sources.label: Livius.org
sources.why: Ancient history articles. Narrative style. Greek, Roman, Persian, Indian civilisations. Free.

sources.label: People's Archive of Rural India
sources.why: Rural India stories, traditions, crafts, oral histories. Unique cross-domain content. Free.

sources.label: The Heritage Lab
sources.why: Art and culture stories from Indian museums. Narrative essays. Free.

sources.label: Internet Archive - Scientific American
sources.why: 1845-1909 Scientific American free archive. Industrial revolution, patent chronicles. Free no login.

sources.label: Internet Archive - National Geographic
sources.why: 1888 onwards National Geographic. Free public domain early volumes. Exploration, science, culture.

sources.label: Illustrated Weekly of India Archive
sources.why: Post-colonial India narrative journalism. Emergency period, social debates, cultural essays. Free.

sources.label: Parry Game Preserve - Discover
sources.why: Free full Discover Magazine issues 1981-1982. Science stories in HTML format. No paywall.

sources.label: Science and Media Museum
sources.why: History of cinema from Kinetoscope to digital. Sound, colour, widescreen. Narrative and readable. Free.

sources.label: Filmmaker IQ
sources.why: Cinema technology history. Lenses, frame rates, continuity editing, greenscreen evolution. Free.

sources.label: Media History Digital Library
sources.why: Historical film magazines including American Cinematographer from 1920s. Camera models, lighting, sound. Free.

sources.label: Cinepedia - Digital Cinema History
sources.why: Early history of digital cinema, projector tech, 1999 digital releases. Narrative. Free.

sources.label: Film Colors - Color in Cinema
sources.why: History of colour in film. Hand-colouring, tinting, Technicolor. Technical evolution. Free.

sources.label: Food Timeline
sources.why: Timelines and narrative notes on food origins and culinary history. Excellent cross-domain source. Free.

sources.label: JSTOR Daily - Food
sources.why: Short essays on food history and social history. Research-backed but written for general readers. Free.

sources.label: The Conversation - Food History
sources.why: Accessible essays by researchers on food history. Journalistic tone. Free.

sources.label: Smarthistory
sources.why: Award-winning narrative essays on architecture, design, craft from ancient to modern. Free open resource.

sources.label: Met Museum - Heilbrunn Timeline
sources.why: Museum-written essays on architectural periods and context. Free.

sources.label: Smithsonian Folklife Magazine
sources.why: Music traditions, cultural transmission, craft heritage. Readable stories. Free.

sources.label: Cooper Hewitt Design Stories
sources.why: Smithsonian Design Museum. Short readable essays on design objects and contexts. Free.

sources.label: V&A Blog
sources.why: Victoria and Albert Museum. Design history, craft processes, conservation stories. Free.

sources.label: Wikipedia Did You Know RSS
sources.why: Wikipedia Did You Know. Daily surprising facts from every domain. Pure wow-factor content. Free.

sources.label: Wikipedia Featured Articles
sources.why: Wikipedia's best articles. In-depth, peer-reviewed, covers every domain. Free.

sources.label: Project Gutenberg New Releases
sources.why: Public domain books newly released. History, science, philosophy, biography. Free no login.

sources.label: The Conversation - History
sources.why: Academic essays written for general readers. History, dark history, social history. Free.

sources.label: The Conversation - Society
sources.why: Society, identity, behaviour essays. Readable academic style. Free.

sources.label: NASA Science
sources.why: Astronomy, earth science stories. Narrative style. Free public resource.

free_apis.name: GNews API
free_apis.notes: Keyword search across global sources. Returns title + description + URL. 5 keywords per run, randomised.

free_apis.name: Mediastack
free_apis.notes: Filter by country=in, language=en. 7500+ sources. No credit card for free tier.

free_apis.name: NewsAPI.org
free_apis.notes: Dev tier only. Research and inspiration only. Cannot use for commercial publishing.
